records.ety: [L. attenuatus, p. p. of attenuare; ad + tenuare to make thin, tenuis thin. See Thin.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Attenuated (<?/); p. pr. & vb. n. Attenuating (<?/).]

defs: To make thin or slender, as by mechanical or chemical action upon inanimate objects, or by the effects of starvation, disease, etc., upon living bodies.
To make thin or less consistent; to render less viscid or dense; to rarefy. Specifically: To subtilize, as the humors of the body, or to break them into finer parts.
To lessen the amount, force, or value of; to make less complex; to weaken.
quotes: To undersell our rivals . . . has led the manufacturer to . . . attenuate his processes, in the allotment of tasks, to an extreme point.
We may reject and reject till we attenuate history into sapless meagerness.

defs: To become thin, slender, or fine; to grow less; to lessen.
quotes: The attention attenuates as its sphere contracts.
